case_name: John Armour, Individually and as Personal Representative of the Estate of Judith Armour v. David Bader, M.D.

summary.brief: John Armour, Individually and as Personal Representative of the Estate of Judith Armour v. David Bader, M.D.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: On November 8, 2016, Judith Armour (Mrs. Armour or the decedent) arrived at South County Cardiology for a stress echocardiogram
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The results of Mrs. Armour’s echocardiogram were “markedly abnormal” and indicated that she “may have significant coronary [artery] disease.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Tragically, the next day, Mrs. Armour died from a heart attack.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Doctor Bouchard testified that Mrs. Armour was referred for a stress test by her primary-care physician because “she was having issues with shortness of breath
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: Doctor Bouchard testified that the changes in Mrs. Armour’s EKG levels were concerning and the fact that it took over seventeen minutes for these changes to resolve was another “red-flag thing[]
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: For the foregoing reasons, the judgment of the Superior Court is vacated. The papers of the case are remanded to the Superior Court for a new trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Referred for a stress test due to issues with shortness of breath and potential coronary blockage.

focused_exam.cardiovascular: Shortness of breath during stress test, no chest pain reported

critical_findings: Shortness of breath during stress test

requestables.name: Stress Echocardiogram
requestables.description: Echocardiogram performed during exercise to assess heart function

reveal.result_summary: Markedly abnormal, indicating potential significant coronary artery disease
reveal.clinical_significance: Suggests high risk of coronary artery disease
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt immediate further evaluation or emergency care

requestables.name: Cardiology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with cardiologist following stress test

reveal.result_summary: Patient was sent home after being deemed stable
reveal.clinical_significance: Decision to send home despite abnormal test results
reveal.decision_impact: Contributed to lack of immediate intervention

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents for stress test due to shortness of breath

key_events.event: Patient arrives at South County Cardiology
Stress test performed, results markedly abnormal

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with abnormal stress test results
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management after an abnormal stress test?

options.description: Send patient to the emergency room for further evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care for high-risk stress test results

options.description: Send patient home with instructions to follow up
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient was deemed stable at the time

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Patient returned to baseline and was stable

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been sent to the emergency room
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was the standard of care

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to act on high-risk stress test results
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from a heart attack the day after the stress test

key_findings: Defendants were found not liable by the jury
Judgment vacated and remanded for a new trial

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to send patient to emergency room
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate intervention could have prevented cardiac arrest

factual_timeline.event: Patient arrives at South County Cardiology for stress test
Patient dies from a heart attack

allegations.allegation: Failure to perform adequate follow-up after stress test

court_findings.legal_significance: Judgment vacated for a new trial
